STT 2, 321 [Šumma Alu 022-023][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

[...] x x x x KA x SAG.DU x [...]

(o i 1') [...] ... head ... [...].

o i 2'2'

[...] ana SIG₅ti₃ u₃ NIG₂.TUK UŠ x [...]

(o i 2') [...] will be led to good fortune and riches [...].

o i 3'3'

[...] ana IGI NA i-mut ŠUBat EN DU₁₁-šu₂ IGImar

(o i 3') [...] dies in front of a man: he will see his enemy's fall.

o i 4'4'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana IGI NA it-ta-pa-aṣ EN DU₁₁-šu₂ GAZak

(o i 4') (If) a snake gets crushed in front of a man: he will kill his enemy.

o i 5'5'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana IGI NA is-ku-up mi-li₃ u ki-šit-ti ŠU

(o i 5') (If) a snake lies down in front of a man: flood1 and conquest (in the) hand.

o i 6'6'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana IGI NA DU.DU mi-li₃

(o i 6') (If) a snake roams around in front of a man: flood.

o i 7'7'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana IGI NA KAŠ₄um KURad₂ A₂.AŠ₂

(o i 7') (If) a snake moves fast in front of a man: achieving a wish.

o i 8'8'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana IGI NA GUB!iz KURti! ŠU LUGAL

(o i 8') (If) a snake stands (still) in front of a man: conquest for the king.

o i 9'9'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana IGI NA im-ṣur te-šu-u₂

(o i 9') (If) a snake moves around in front of a man: chaos.

o i 10'10'

[* MUŠ-MEŠ] ana IGI NA GIB-MEŠ DU₁₄ UŠ-MEŠ-šu₂

(o i 10') [(If) snakes] lie across in front of a man: conflict will continuously accompany him.

o i 11'11'

[* MUŠ-MEŠ] ana IGI NA kit-pu-lu ina-an-ziq

(o i 11') (If) [snakes] are entwined in front of a man: he will become annoyed.

o i 12'12'

[* MUŠ] ana IGI NA i-tak-kal ⸢ZI⸣bu kaš-du

(o i 12') (If) [a snake] keeps eating in front of a man: successful attack.

o i 13'13'

[...] ana ⸢IGI NA⸣ x ⸢ZI?⸣ [...] ⸢GA?⸣ ŠU₂ GU₇

(o i 13') (If) [...] ... in front of a man: he will eat ....

o i 14'14'

[...] ana IGI NA x [...] x BU DU im-ma-gar

(o i 14') (If) [...] ... in front of a man: he will make an agreement ....

o i 15'15'

[...] ana IGI NA SA [...] MAR ina la₃ DU₁₀ ŠA₃ DU-MEŠ

(o i 15') (If) [...] ... in front of a man: [...] he will roam around unsatisfied.

o i 16'16'

[...] ana IGI NA I [...] x i-ša-giš [x]

(o i 16') (If) [...] ... in front of a man: [...] ... will kill ....

o i 17'17'

[...] ana IGI NA [...] x [...]

(o i 17') (If) [...] in front of a man: ... [...].

o i 18'18'

[...] KAṢ₃ ṢA [...]

(o i 18') [...] ... [...].

o i 19'19'

(o i 19') [...] ... city ... [...] they will experience evil.

o i 20'20'

[...] NA x [...] ⸢E₂?⸣ u EME-šu₂ E₃-MEŠ NA BI DAB-ma GAZ

(o i 20') [...] a man ... [...] ... and its tongue keeps going out: that man will be seized and killed.

o i 21'21'

[...] x EN DU₁₁-šu₂ ina la₃ DU₁₀ub UZU DU-MEŠ

(o i 21') [...] ... his enemy; he will roam around in bad physical health.

o i 22'22'

[...] KURad₂ A₂.AŠ₂

(o i 22') [...]: achieving a wish.

o i 23'23'

[...] ŠUBut KURad₂ A₂.AŠ₂

(o i 23') [...] falls: achieving a wish.

o i 24'24'

[...] ŠUBut mu-kil ku-tal-li LU₂ UŠ₂

(o i 24') [...] falls: the man's supporter will die.

o i 25'25'

[...] x NA ŠUBut DUMU-MEŠ-šu₂ UŠ₂-MEŠ

(o i 25') [...] falls (on) the man's ...: his sons will die.

o i 26'26'

[...] ⸢NA⸣ ŠUBut lu NIG₂.TUK UKU₂ lu MAŠ.EN.GAG SIG₅ <<IGI>> NA BI LIL₂aʾ

(o i 26') [...] falls (on) a man's [...]: either the rich man will become poor or the poor man will prosper; that man will become infected.

o i 27'27'

[...] x NA ⸢GU₄⸣.U₄-ma ana KI ŠUBut ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃<(lu)> NIG₂.TUK UKU₂ lu MAŠ.EN.GAG SIG₅ NA BI LIL₂aʾ

(o i 27') [...] jumps (on) a man's [...] and falls on the ground: ditto (either the rich man will become poor or the poor man will prosper); that man will become infected.

o i 28'28'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ x [...] x x UD ⸢ZI⸣bu kaš-du

(o i 28') (If) a snake ... [...] ...: successful attack.

o i 29'29'

* ⸢MUŠ⸣ ana UGU [...] ŠUBat EN DU₁₁-šu₂ IGImar

(o i 29') (If) a snake [...] on top of [...]: he will see his enemy's fall.

o i 30'30'

* MUŠ ana UGU [...] ŠUBti₃ ⸢ARAD⸣ u [...]

(o i 30') (If) a snake [...] on top of [...]: a servant's stroke and ... [...].

o i 31'31'

* MUŠ ana UGU [...] x

(o i 31') (If) a snake [...] on top of [...]: ... [...].

o i 32'32'

* MUŠ ana UGU NA x x ⸢BI⸣ ša₂-gi-šu₂ [...]

(o i 32') (If) a snake ... on top of a man: ... a murderer [...].

o ii 44

o ii 66

o ii 88

[...] GIG ⸢ŠUBut⸣ GIG-su GID₂.DA TIuṭ

[...] GIG ŠUBut ina x x TA BA.UŠ₂

(o ii 10) [...] falls (on) a sick person, whose illness has been long: his illness will leave him.

r i 8'8'

(r i 8') (If) snakes are entwined like a string in a man's house and are seen: that household will be scattered; death ... [...].

r i 9'9'

* MUŠ-MEŠ ina E₂ NA u₂-sa-di-ru-⸢ma?⸣ ik-tap-pi-li E₂ BI x [...]

(r i 9') (If) snakes, in a man's house, keep entertwining all the time: that house [...].

r i 10'10'

* ⸢MUŠ⸣-[MEŠ] [(x)] x DABtu₄ TA x [x] x MEŠ ŠUB-MEŠ GIŠ ⸢GAB?⸣-MEŠ E₂ BI x

(r i 10') (If) snakes, (which) seize [...], fall from the ... [...] ...: the ... of that house ....

r i 11'11'

* MUŠ-MEŠ MIN-ma U₂ x KU [...] gišUR₃-MEŠ ŠUB-MEŠ IGI-MEŠ x [(x)]

(r i 11') (If) snakes, (which) ditto and ..., [...] fall (from) the beams (and) are seen ... [...].

r i 12'12'

* MUŠ-MEŠ ana NA i?-ku-pu-ma tap-pa-šu₂ ⸢i?⸣-lu-u₄ E₂ BI DAB-šu DINGIR DAB-šu

(r i 12') (If) snakes come close to a man and mount their mate: that house will be seized from him; the god will seize him.

r i 13'13'

* MUŠ-MEŠ ana IGI NA is-kup [...] NA BI BA.[UŠ₂]

(r i 13') (If) snakes lie down in front of a man [...]: that man will die.

r i 14'14'

[*] MUŠ 3 SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI [...] x SI.⸢SA₂⸣

(r i 14') (If) a 3-headed snake is seen [...]: ... will go well.

r i 15'15'

[*] ⸢MUŠ 4⸣ SAG.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI ⸢NA BI⸣ ana ITI 1-KAM₂ x [...]

(r i 15') (If) a 4-headed snake is seen: that man, for 1 month ... [...].

r i 16'16'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ [x] ⸢SAG⸣.DU-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI NA ⸢BI?⸣ ina? ITI 3-KAM₂ x [...]

(r i 16') (If) a [...]-headed snake is seen: that man, in the third month ... [...].

r i 17'17'

[*] MUŠ 2 SAG.DU-⸢MEŠ-šu₂⸣ <<IGI>> ina IRI u ⸢A.GAR₃⸣ IGI LU₂ la KUR x

(r i 17') (If) a 2-headed snake is seen in the city and in the meadow: a man who is not of the land [...], will seize the throne; that city and the meadow will be abandoned.

r i 18'18'

[x (x)] gišGU.ZA DABbat IRI BI u A.⸢GAR₃⸣ <<u>> ŠUBdi

r i 19'19'

[*] ⸢MUŠ⸣ ina E₂ DINGIR IGI NIDBA [...] GAL₂

(r i 19') (If) a snake is seen in a god's temple: there will be a [...] food offering.

r i 20'20'

[*] MUŠ-MEŠ [...] ik-tap-pi-lu GIŠ [...] ME PAD

(r i 20') (If) snakes keep intertwining [...] ....

r i 21'21'

[*] MUŠ ina E₂ DINGIR ⸢SA.A⸣ GU₇ x [...] BA.UŠ₂

(r i 21') (If) a snake eats a cat in a god's temple ... [...] will die.

r i 22'22'

[*] MUŠ ina E₂ DINGIR ina UGU [...] ⸢TI⸣

(r i 22') (If) a snake [...] on top of [...] in a god's temple: [...] he will live.

r i 23'23'

[...] ina SAG.DU DINGIR ⸢GAL₂⸣ [...] SU BAL-su

(r i 23') [...] there is [...] on the god's head: [...] ... will revolt against him.

r i 24'24'

[...] x [...] NUN UŠ₂

(r i 24') [...]: a prince will die.

r i 25'25'

[...] NUN LA₂mu

(r i 25') [...]: a prince will be captured.

r i 26'26'

[...] x DINGIR x [...] ⸢NIDBA⸣ URU₄iš

(r i 26') [...] ... god ... [...]: a food offering will be requested.

r i 27'27'

[...] x MI KUR us₂!-sur-tu₄ DABbat

(r i 27') [...] ...: a siege will afflict the land.

r i 28'28'

[...] gišBANŠUR? DINGIR NU₂ x x MA KA NA A MAḪ-ME

(r i 28') [...] lies down (on) a god's offering table ... : ... they will be exalted

r i 29'29'

[...] x x ⸢NUN?⸣ i-mut NUN BI ina MU ⸢BI⸣ BA.⸢UŠ₂⸣

(r i 29') [...] dies ... a prince: that prince will die during that year.

r i 30'30'

[...] IRI kap-ri u na-me-e [...] x x NA x DU

(r i 30') [...] (in) the city, the village and the steppe ... [...] a man ... will go [...] ... that city ... [...] will experience ....

r i 31'31'

[...] ⸢IB⸣ SAL IRI BI x x [...] ⸢ŠU⸣ LAM ŠI SI x ⸢IGI⸣mar

Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

[...] x NA x [...] E₂ BI a-ši-bu-šu NU LIBIRra-MEŠ

(r ii 1) [...] ... a man ... [...]: the inhabitants of that house will not live long.

r ii 22

[...] ⸢E₂⸣ NA MIN x [...] UM GAL₂u₂ EN E₂ UŠ₂

(r ii 2) [...] there are [...] a man's house ...: the owner of the house will die.

r ii 33

[...] E₂ NA ⸢EME⸣-MIN-⸢šu₂⸣ [x] x E₂ BI SAG.KALtu₂ DU DUMU-MEŠ TUKU

(r ii 3) (If) [a snake] (with) 2 tongues [...] ... (in) a man's house: that house will gain pre-eminence; he will have sons.

r ii 44

[...] ⸢E₂ NA ša₂?⸣ EME-MIN-šu₂ IGI x A ŠUB-MEŠ E₂ BI ⸢BIR⸣ ER₂ TILti

(r ii 4) (If) [a snake] that (has) 2 tongues is seen (in) a man's house (and) keeps falling ...: that household will be scattered; mourning will cease.

r ii 55

[...] ⸢NU₂⸣ UŠ₂ut nu-kur₂ gišNU₂-šu₂-ma <<NU₂>> ZI.GA ḪULti

(r ii 5) [...] dies (in) a bed: demolishing of his bed and exit of evil.

r ii 66

[...] NA ir-bi-iṣ EN x BI ⸢ana gišGU⸣.ZA-šu₂ ZIbe₂

(r ii 6) [...] lies down [...] of a man: the owner of that ... will get up from his chair.

r ii 77

[...] UŠ₂ut ina x [...] MAN-ma [...] ⸢ŠU⸣ [x] x

(r ii 7) [...] die: in ... [...] will become change and [...] ....

r ii 88

[...] NA ir-bi-iṣ EN E₂ GIG dan-na DAB [x]

(r ii 8) [...] lies down [...] of a man: the owner of the house will be seized (with) a severe illness.

r ii 99

[...] x NI IM [...] ⸢E₂⸣ DINGIR BI NIDBA KU₅⸢as⸣

(r ii 9) [...]: the food offering will cease (in) the temple of that god.

r ii 1010

[...] x ŠU₂ DAGAL

(r ii 10) [...] ... will increase.

r ii 1111

[...] x AN ⸢TU?⸣ [...] x NU TI GUR-MEŠ-šu₂

(r ii 11) [...] ... [...] ... they will return to him.

r ii 1212

[...] ŠU₂ KUR x [...] DAGALeš

(r ii 12) [...] ... the land ... [...] will increase.

r ii 1313

[...] x MU ina ḫi-rit IRI IGIir [...] x RA

(r ii 13) [...] ... is seen in the city's ditch: [...] ... will devastate.

r ii 1414

[...] x ina E₂.⸢GAL⸣ LUGAL IGIir [...] x

(r ii 14) [...] ... is seen in the king's palace [...] ....

r ii 1515

(r ii 15) [...] ... arises and ... and [...].

(r ii 18) [...] ... non canonical ... [...]

r ii 1919

(r ii 19) [...] the city and in front of [...]: the city [...] will be abandoned.

[...] x MEŠ ina E₂ BI [x] ŠUBdi

(r ii 20) [...] ... in that house [...] will be abandoned.

r ii 2121

[...] NA IGI-MEŠ x ⸢SUḪUŠ?⸣ [...]

r ii 2222

[...] x x x [...] ⸢IRI⸣ IM TI x IRI BI [...]

r ii 2323

[...] NA PEŠ₂ iṣ-bat x UZU ana E₂ BI [...]

r ii 2424

[...] x MUŠ iṣ-bat ⸢NA⸣ [...] ⸢DUMU-MEŠ⸣-šu₂ [...]

[...] x [...]

(r ii 26) [...] ... [...]

(rest of column missing)

1 The scribe has apparently confused mīlu "flood " and lītu "booty " here . lītu and kiššitu are commonly attested together (CAD K : 452).

Created by Marie-Françoise Besnier for the AHRC-funded GKAB Project, 2009 and released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/cams/gkab/P338636
